title: SQL JOINs and Aggregation
summary: Combine multiple tables using JOIN and summarize data with GROUP BY, COUNT, SUM, AVG.
content_html:
SQL joins combine data from multiple tables, and aggregate functions summarize data.

INNER JOIN

SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
INNER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
-- Returns only rows with matching keys in both tables

LEFT JOIN

SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
-- Returns ALL employees, NULLs for unmatched departments

Other Joins

  • RIGHT JOIN: all rows from right table
  • FULL OUTER JOIN: all rows from both tables

Aggregate Functions

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees;
SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees;
SELECT SUM(amount) FROM orders;
SELECT MAX(salary), MIN(salary) FROM employees;

GROUP BY

SELECT department, COUNT(*), AVG(salary)
FROM employees
GROUP BY department
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;  -- filter groups (not rows)

exercises.title: INNER JOIN
options: Only rows with matching values in both tables
All rows from the left table
exercises.answer: Only rows with matching values in both tables

exercises.title: LEFT JOIN
options: All rows from the left table, NULLs for unmatched right table rows
All rows from the right table
exercises.answer: All rows from the left table, NULLs for unmatched right table rows

exercises.title: COUNT
options: Total number of rows in orders
exercises.answer: Total number of rows in orders

exercises.title: GROUP BY purpose
options: Group rows by a column and apply aggregate functions per group
exercises.answer: Group rows by a column and apply aggregate functions per group

exercises.title: JOIN ON clause

options: The condition for matching rows between tables

exercises.title: Alias
exercises.prompt: In 'FROM employees e', what does 'e' do?
options: Creates a short alias for the table name
exercises.answer: Creates a short alias for the table name
